steps.title: Evaluate the formula at the boundaries of the training intensity range
steps.content:
At $p = 0.5$ (minimum training intensity):
$$h = 120(0.5) + 60 = 60 + 60 = 120 \text{ bpm}$$

At $p = 0.85$ (maximum training intensity):
$$h = 120(0.85) + 60 = 102 + 60 = 162 \text{ bpm}$$

steps.title: Express the training heart rate as an inequality
steps.content:
Since $h = 120p + 60$ is linear with positive slope, the training heart rate range directly corresponds to the intensity range $0.5 \leq p \leq 0.85$:
$$120 \leq h \leq 162$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
